<!--
  omiiCARE_QA Pull Request
  Keep PRs small and focused: one logical change per PR (see CONTRIBUTING.md).
  Commit messages follow Conventional Commits. Target branch is usually `develop`.
-->

## Summary

<!-- What does this PR do and why? 2–5 sentences. State the user/engineering value. -->

## Related Issue

<!-- Link the issue this PR closes or relates to. Use a keyword to auto-close. -->

Closes #
Related to #

## Type of Change

<!-- Mark all that apply with an [x]. -->

- [ ] `feat` — New feature
- [ ] `fix` — Bug fix
- [ ] `docs` — Documentation only
- [ ] `refactor` — Code change that neither fixes a bug nor adds a feature
- [ ] `test` — Adding or correcting tests
- [ ] `chore` — Build, tooling, or maintenance
- [ ] `perf` — Performance improvement
- [ ] `ci` — CI/CD configuration
- [ ] Breaking change (describe migration impact below)

## Milestone

<!-- Which roadmap milestone does this PR serve? It MUST stay within that
     milestone's fence (see ROADMAP.md). Mark exactly one. -->

- [ ] M1 — Foundation, Architecture & Governance
- [ ] M2 — Infrastructure & Environment Foundation
- [ ] M3 — Healthcare Platform Core (Backend)
- [ ] M4 — Frontend Platform & Portals
- [ ] M5 — Quality Engineering Platform (Automation)
- [ ] M6 — Manual Quality Engineering Assets
- [ ] M7 — Advanced Quality Engineering
- [ ] M8 — DevOps, CI/CD & Release Engineering
- [ ] M9 — AI-Native Quality Engineering
- [ ] M10 — Production Hardening & Release 1.0.0

## Definition of Done Checklist

<!-- Every box must be checked or explicitly marked N/A with a reason.
     Maps to docs/DEFINITION_OF_DONE.md. -->

- [ ] **Build** — Project builds cleanly with no new warnings.
- [ ] **Tests** — New/changed code is covered by tests; all tests pass locally.
- [ ] **Coverage** — Coverage meets or exceeds the threshold; no regression.
- [ ] **Docs** — Documentation updated (READMEs, guides, blueprints affected).
- [ ] **ADR** — A significant architectural decision is recorded as an ADR (or N/A).
- [ ] **Lint/Format** — Lint, format, and static analysis pass (Spotless/Checkstyle/PMD/SpotBugs or FE equivalents).
- [ ] **Security** — No secrets committed; dependency/security scan clean; OWASP considerations reviewed.
- [ ] **Accessibility** — UI changes meet WCAG AA (stable selectors, labels, contrast) or N/A.
- [ ] **Performance** — No obvious performance regression; perf-sensitive paths checked or N/A.
- [ ] **No dead code** — No commented-out, unreachable, placeholder, or TODO code remains.
- [ ] **Milestone fence** — Change stays within the active milestone's scope (ROADMAP.md).
- [ ] **PHI-safe** — Only synthetic, PHI-safe data is used; no real patient data.
- [ ] **Conventional Commits** — Commit messages follow the convention.

## Screenshots / Evidence

<!-- Attach screenshots, logs, test reports (Allure/Extent), or recordings that
     demonstrate the change. Required for UI and test-execution changes. -->

## Reviewer Notes

<!-- Anything reviewers should focus on: risky areas, design tradeoffs (cite ADRs),
     follow-ups deliberately left out of scope, or questions for the reviewer. -->
